regions.us-ports: Today in Miami I am tracking NW 2kt winds with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, and I am highlighting Port Everglades and Port Canaveral as nearby ports to watch for the active thunderstorms now. For operations today I expect a Day 1 forecast of SE 9kt with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability at Miami, which could prompt brief adjustments to embarkation and pier-side excursion timing at Miami and Port Everglades. Over the next 24 to 48 hours the Day 2 outlook at Miami shows S 10kt winds with thunderstorms and less than 10% rain probability, so I will coordinate ship departure and tender schedules with Port Canaveral and Tampa Bay around thunderstorm windows. Beyond 48 hours the Day 3 forecast turns SSE 10kt with thunderstorms and 10% rain probability at Miami, and I will continue monitoring for persistent thunderstorms that could require changes to tendering or deck activity at Miami, Port Everglades, and Port Canaveral. I will issue operational updates if winds diverge from the SE/S/SSE 9 to 10kt trend or if the thunderstorms noted at Miami with less than 10% rain probability (Day 1–2) or 10% rain probability (Day 3) intensify during embarkation or disembarkation windows.
